title: Blocked-state — orthogonal stuck flag with provenance for work items
scope:
Introduce a blocked state on work items that is independent of flow state. A work item can be blocked anywhere in its flow (Todo, Doing, In Review, Done-pending-verification, etc.) — being blocked tells us the item is stuck, but says nothing about where it got stuck. Recording it as a separate orthogonal flag (rather than a flow-state value) preserves the flow position, which is the only signal that tells us where the blockage exists in the workflow.

Schema additions on the artefacts table (all nullable except is_blocked): is_blocked BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE, blocked_id UUID (surrogate id of the block episode, so multiple block/unblock cycles are addressable), blocked_title TEXT, blocked_description TEXT, blocked_reason TEXT, blocked_user_reporter UUID, blocked_user_unblocked UUID, blocked_date_blocked TIMESTAMPTZ, blocked_date_unblocked TIMESTAMPTZ.

Surfaces affected: backend service (Block/Unblock methods + notifier events), v2 API (POST /work-items/{id}/block + /unblock), UI (action on detail panel, badge on row + tree node, tab/panel banner), webhooks (item.blocked + item.unblocked events already wired into the dropdown via PLA-0036/B9.7), and reports (blocked-time excluded from cycle/lead time, or surfaced as a separate metric).
value: Today, teams have no first-class way to mark a work item as stuck without lying about its flow position. Forcing 'blocked' to be a flow state collapses two orthogonal facts (where it is, and whether it can move) into one column, which destroys reporting fidelity and forces process workarounds. Modelling blocked as an orthogonal flag preserves both signals: cycle-time can subtract blocked time, dashboards can show blockages by flow column, and webhooks can fire item.blocked / item.unblocked events without state-transition noise. The provenance fields (reporter, unblocker, dates, reason, free-form description) give support and process-improvement teams the audit trail they need.
implementation_plan: Task 1: Migration — add 9 blocked_* columns to vector_artefacts.artefacts (one ordered SQL file under db/artefacts_schema/).
Task 2: Backend — extend artefacts service with Block(itemID, reporterID, title, description, reason) and Unblock(itemID, unblockerID) methods; both fire notifier events.
Task 3: Backend — wire item.blocked and item.unblocked notifier events into the webhook fan-out path (mirror item.status_changed wiring).
Task 4: Backend — POST /samantha/v2/work-items/{id}/block and /unblock route handlers; permission gate on workspace.write or equivalent.
Task 5: Backend — update openapi-v2.yaml with both new paths and the blocked_* response shape on the existing work-item detail.
Task 6: Frontend — add Block/Unblock action to the work-item detail panel; modal captures title/description/reason on Block.
Task 7: Frontend — visible block marker on rows (Table component badge), tree nodes (ResourceTree decoration), and detail panel (banner above flow state).
Task 8: Frontend — webhook dropdown additions (item.blocked, item.unblocked) — already added to WebhookForm.tsx as part of PLA-0036/B9.7 placeholder; verify.
Task 9: Reports — surface blocked time as a separate metric and provide cycle-time variant that subtracts blocked intervals.
Task 10: Tests — unit (Block/Unblock service idempotency), API (route mounts + permission gate), Playwright E2E (block flow → badge appears → unblock clears it).
Task 11: Docs — Vector_Scope.md B1.8 hand-off note; docs/c_c_blocked_state.md with schema, service surface, UI rules.
areas_impacted: Backend: backend/internal/artefacts/service.go (or equivalent) — Block + Unblock methods
Backend: backend/internal/notifier — item.blocked, item.unblocked event topics
Spec: openapi-v2.yaml — two new paths + blocked_* fields on the work-item schema
Frontend: app/(user)/work-items/* — detail panel action + badge; tree decoration
Frontend: app/components/Table.tsx adopters — block badge on rows
Frontend: app/(user)/workspace-settings/webhooks/WebhookForm.tsx — already lists item.blocked; add item.unblocked
Reports: any cycle-time or lead-time report — blocked-interval subtraction
feature_list: is_blocked boolean column with default FALSE on artefacts
blocked_id surrogate UUID — addressable per block episode
item.blocked + item.unblocked notifier events fired on transition
Detail-panel Block/Unblock action with reason modal
Table row badge showing blocked status
ResourceTree node decoration showing blocked status
Detail-panel blocked banner with reason + reporter + since-date
Webhook dropdown options for item.blocked + item.unblocked
Reports — cycle-time variant subtracting blocked intervals
features_extended: Work-item detail panel — gains a new Block/Unblock action and banner
Webhook subscriptions (PLA-0007 era / B9 family) — gain two new event types
Cycle-time / lead-time reports — gain blocked-time awareness
openapi-v2.yaml — gains two new paths and 9 schema fields

B1.8.2 Backend — Block/Unblock service methods on artefacts service; idempotent; fire notifier events
B1.8.2a Backend — wire item.blocked + item.unblocked notifier events into webhook fan-out
B1.8.3 API — POST /samantha/v2/work-items/{id}/block route handler with permission gate
B1.8.3a API — POST /samantha/v2/work-items/{id}/unblock route handler with permission gate
B1.8.3b Spec — update openapi-v2.yaml with /block + /unblock paths and blocked_* schema fields
B1.8.4 UI — Block/Unblock action on work-item detail panel; modal captures title/description/reason on Block
B1.8.4a UI — blocked badge on Table rows (row decoration via Table component primitives)
B1.8.4b UI — blocked decoration on ResourceTree nodes
B1.8.4c UI — blocked banner on detail panel (reason + reporter + since-date)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Migration applies cleanly on dev (vector_artefacts.artefacts has all 9 blocked_* columns); is_blocked defaults to FALSE for existing rows
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: psql \d artefacts inspection; SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE is_blocked = false matches total row count

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Block(itemID, reporterID, title, description, reason) sets is_blocked=true, populates 5 provenance fields + blocked_date_blocked, leaves flow_state untouched, and fires item.blocked notifier event
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: unit test on artefacts service; manual SQL inspection after API call

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Unblock(itemID, unblockerID) sets is_blocked=false, populates blocked_user_unblocked + blocked_date_unblocked, and fires item.unblocked notifier event; provenance fields remain for audit
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: unit test + SQL inspection — old blocked_title/reason still present after unblock

acceptance_criteria.criterion: POST /samantha/v2/work-items/{id}/block and /unblock routes respond 200 on success, 403 without permission, 404 on unknown id, 409 on duplicate transition (block-when-blocked, unblock-when-unblocked)
Webhook subscribers receive item.blocked and item.unblocked events with full payload (item id + 9 blocked_* fields)
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: manual webhook trigger against a local listener; verify payload matches openapi-v2 schema

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Work-item detail panel shows Block action when not blocked, Unblock action when blocked; modal captures title/description/reason on Block; banner shows reason + reporter + since-date when blocked
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: manual browser test on the work-items detail panel

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Blocked items show a visible badge on Table rows AND ResourceTree nodes; flow_state column unchanged
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: manual browser test — block an item in Doing column, verify badge renders without state change

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Cycle-time report exposes blocked-time as a separate series; cycle-time-excluding-blocked variant available
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: manual report inspection on a tenant with at least one blocked-then-unblocked item

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Playwright E2E spec passes: open item → Block (with reason) → badge appears → Unblock → badge clears; flow_state never changes
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: CI green on the new spec

risks.risk: Schema migration on populated artefacts table — adding 9 columns to a large table can lock the row for a long time on PG
risks.mitigation: All columns nullable except is_blocked (which has DEFAULT FALSE — fast path on PG 11+); test on dev seed (~55k rows) and time the migration; document any required maintenance window for prod cutover

risks.risk: UI confusion — users may think 'blocked' is a flow state, leading to mis-modelled processes
risks.mitigation: Banner above (not within) the flow-state pill; documentation explicitly contrasts blocked-vs-state; modal copy reinforces 'item is stuck, flow position unchanged'

risks.risk: Reports — blocked-time subtraction logic is non-trivial when block episodes overlap state transitions
risks.mitigation: Use blocked_date_blocked / blocked_date_unblocked as the authoritative interval; subtract from total cycle-time per item; document edge cases (item moved while blocked, multiple block episodes once we add history)

risks.risk: Single-episode model — blocked_id + 9 columns capture only the CURRENT block episode; historical episodes are overwritten on re-block
risks.mitigation: Phase 1 ships single-episode (matches user spec); follow-up plan can add artefact_block_history table if multi-episode reporting becomes a requirement

risks.risk: Webhook fan-out — item.blocked event must NOT also fire item.updated (or vice versa), or subscribers will double-process
risks.mitigation: Block/Unblock methods fire ONLY the specific event; verified by integration test that subscribes to both topics and asserts no overlap

risks.risk: Permission model — should blocking require workspace.write, or a more specific workspace.block permission?
risks.mitigation: Phase 1 reuses workspace.write (lowest friction); follow-up if tenants ask for finer gating (PLA-0007 RBAC supports adding the code without schema change)

references.label: docs/c_c_table_component.md — row badge primitive for blocked decoration
docs/c_c_resource_tree.md — node decoration for blocked tree nodes
